vision_report.compositional_truth_check: The biome is currently in a state of advanced senescence. P3 (Pothos/Rabbit) shows significant leaf necrosis. P1 (String of Nickels) is present but showing signs of dehydration. P2/P4 (Mexican Mint/Silver Guest) are largely indistinguishable as distinct healthy specimens, appearing as a singular, struggling cluster.

inventory_reconciliation.P1: Present, but showing signs of chlorosis and potential dehydration.
inventory_reconciliation.P2: Systemic Loss/High Stress; specimen is barely visible/shriveled.
inventory_reconciliation.P3: Present; anchor (rabbit) is stable, but primary foliage is necrotic.
inventory_reconciliation.P4: Systemic Loss; no distinct growth identifiable as Silver Guest.

plant_audit.P1: Yellow pot; foliage is sparse and dull, indicating low vigor.
plant_audit.P2: Black pot; foliage is severely diminished, likely suffering from root zone issues.
plant_audit.P3: Black pot; white rabbit anchor is clear. One leaf is fully necrotic (brown/withered), one leaf remains green but shows signs of edge curling.
plant_audit.P4: Black pot; no viable growth detected.

biome_observations.soil_texture: Soil appears consistently damp/dark, suggesting potential over-watering or lack of drainage, which contradicts the visual signs of plant stress.
biome_observations.debris: White perlite/grit is visible on the surface of all pots. No fungal blooms detected.

temporal_deltas.T-5_to_T-3: Initial observation shows a yellowing leaf on P3. P1 remains stable.
temporal_deltas.T-2_to_T-1: Significant postural collapse of the P3 leaf; color shift from yellow-green to deep brown (necrosis).
temporal_deltas.CURRENT: The necrotic leaf on P3 has fully dried. No new growth observed in any pot.

vision_report.visual_health_inference: The biome is experiencing a failure to thrive. The combination of dark, damp soil and necrotic foliage suggests root rot or a severe nutrient imbalance. The plants are in a state of decline.
vision_report.anomalies: The presence of a white rabbit anchor in P3 is the only non-botanical feature. No new sprouts or incidental growth detected.
vision_report.narrative_description: The botanical collection is currently in a critical state. The Pothos (P3) is undergoing rapid senescence, likely due to substrate saturation. The String of Nickels (P1) is showing signs of stress, and the Mexican Mint (P2) is effectively non-viable. Immediate intervention (re-potting or soil aeration) is recommended to prevent total loss.
